2606.10632.title: Is Fairness Truly Fair? Towards Reliable Lipschitz Fairness in Multi-Task Learning via Fixed-\texorpdfstring{$δ$}{delta} Alignment
2606.10632.paper_summary:
Lipschitz-style individual fairness formalizes the idea that semantically similar examples should receive similar predictions, but its evaluation in multi-task learning (MTL) can be confounded by method-induced representation scales. This paper identifies threshold confounding: when the auditing tolerance is derived from each model's own representation distances, different algorithms are compared under different semantic thresholds. A threshold-drift analysis further shows how Bias rankings can change and identifies sufficient conditions for ranking preservation.
  We propose \textbf{ReLiF}, a reliability-aware framework that separates evaluation-time fixed-$δ$ auditing from training-time controlled regularization. ReLiF uses a shared reference tolerance for comparable auditing and a violation-rate feedback controller to keep the Lipschitz surrogate active without letting it dominate stochastic training. This work also develops supporting analysis for threshold drift, reference-tolerance selection, and the relationship between the huberized training surrogate and its unsmoothed positive-margin counterpart.
  Experiments on clinical time-series benchmarks and NYUv2 (NYU Depth V2) dense prediction show that fixed-$δ$ auditing exposes utility--fairness trade-offs that method-dependent thresholds can obscure. On NYUv2 with a ResNet50 backbone, ReLiF achieves competitive utility while substantially reducing aligned bias under shared fixed thresholds. On clinical benchmarks, ReLiF yields controlled fairness-regularized trade-offs, while fixed-$δ$ auditing reveals that task-balancing baselines can sometimes achieve lower bias and that genuine utility--fairness trade-offs persist. These results support fixed-$δ$ auditing as a semantically consistent protocol for evaluating Lipschitz fairness in MTL.
2606.10632.paper_summary_zh:
Lipschitz風格的個體公平性形式化了語義相似的例子應該獲得相似預測的想法，但在多任務學習（MTL）中的評估可能會受到方法引起的表示尺度的干擾。本文確定了閾值混淆：當審計容忍度是從每個模型自身的表示距離推導出來時，不同算法在不同的語義閾值下進行比較。閾值漂移分析進一步顯示了偏見排名如何改變，並確定了排名保持的充分條件。<lb/>
我們提出了\textbf{ReLiF}，一個關注可靠性的框架，它將評估時固定-$δ$的審計與訓練時的受控正則化分開。ReLiF使用共享的參考容忍度進行可比較的審計，並使用違規率反饋控制器保持Lipschitz代理活躍，而不讓其主導隨機訓練。這項工作還開發了對閾值漂移、參考容忍度選擇以及huber化訓練代理與其未平滑的正邊際對應物之間關係的支持分析。<lb/>
在臨床時間序列基準和NYUv2（NYU Depth V2）密集預測上的實驗顯示，固定-$δ$的審計揭示了效用與公平性之間的權衡，而方法依賴的閾值可能會掩蓋這一點。在使用ResNet50骨幹的NYUv2上，ReLiF在共享固定閾值下實現了競爭性的效用，同時顯著降低了對齊偏見。在臨床基準上，ReLiF產生了受控的公平性正則化權衡，而固定-$δ$的審計則揭示了任務平衡基準有時可以實現較低的偏見，並且真正的效用與公平性之間的權衡仍然存在。這些結果支持固定-$δ$的審計作為在MTL中評估Lipschitz公平性的語義一致協議。
2606.10632.author: Junbo Ding et.al.
2606.10632.authors: Junbo Ding, Xin Zang, Chenchen Pan, Donghao Song, Jiaxin Zhu, Danhuai Guo
